item.title: China Wields Security Laws to Cow Hong Kong’s Once-Free Literary Scene
item.description: Hong Kong once published books on topics such as the Tiananmen crackdown. But new arrests show Beijing intends to bring the city’s booksellers and publishers to heel.

item.title: China Aggressively Patrols Disputed Waters. Now the U.S. Coast Guard Is Moving In.
item.description: Several ships have been pulled from the Middle East to help challenge Beijing’s assertion of power in the Pacific.

item.title: Opinion | Peter Navarro: America Can Break China’s Choke Hold on Critical Minerals
item.description: As we did during Covid, the government is helping U.S. companies to meet national-security needs.

item.title: Former Fed Adviser Gets 38 Months in Prison for Lying to Internal Watchdog
item.description: A jury found John Rogers guilty earlier this year of making a false statement but acquitted him of a more serious charge of conspiracy to commit economic espionage for China.

item.title: China’s Economy Grows at Weakest Pace Since 2022
item.description: The country’s surging exports, buoyed by the artificial-intelligence boom, failed to compensate for a struggling domestic economy and sluggish consumer spending.

item.title: China Holds U.S. Seismologist on Espionage Charges
item.description: Youlin Chen’s research focused on nuclear testing by North Korea.

item.title: He Called Out China’s Economy. Then He Went Silent—for Good.
item.description: Plus, Beijing’s AI ambitions and why some readers don’t trust Chinese EVs

item.title: How Xi Went From Admiring Putin to Managing Him
item.description: Plus, data-center builders are racing to offload stakes worth billions, and the superrich are snapping up neighboring properties to create private compounds.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
